---
title: "Additional Readings"
---

A curated list of the most useful single-cell tutorials, books, papers, and reference websites. These complement the workshop lectures and exercises; none are required reading, but at least one item from each section will repay the time many times over.

## Living best-practices books

Long-form, opinionated guides written by working practitioners. Read at least one cover-to-cover during the workshop.

-   **[Single-cell best practices (Heumos *et al.*)](https://www.sc-best-practices.org/)** --- the canonical online textbook for the field. Strong on rationale, comparisons, and pitfalls. Source for the workshop glossary.
-   **[Orchestrating Single-Cell Analysis with Bioconductor (OSCA)](https://bioconductor.org/books/release/OSCA/)** --- Amezquita, Lun, Bays, Hicks *et al.* The Bioconductor / `SingleCellExperiment` perspective, with running examples.
-   **[Analysis of single-cell RNA-seq data (Sanger / EBI course)](https://www.singlecellcourse.org/)** --- a complete course-style book, free and updated regularly.

## Framework documentation & tutorials

-   **[Seurat tutorials](https://satijalab.org/seurat/articles/)** --- the workshop's primary R framework. Start with the [PBMC 3k guided clustering tutorial](https://satijalab.org/seurat/articles/pbmc3k_tutorial), then look at integration and reference mapping.
-   **[Scanpy tutorials](https://scanpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/tutorials.html)** --- the Python equivalent. The PBMC tutorial mirrors Seurat's so you can compare side-by-side.
-   **[scverse](https://scverse.org/)** --- the Python single-cell ecosystem (AnnData, Scanpy, Muon, scvi-tools, squidpy).
-   **[Signac](https://stuartlab.org/signac/)** --- scATAC-seq in R, Seurat-compatible.
-   **[ArchR](https://www.archrproject.com/)** --- scATAC-seq in R, alternative to Signac; strong visualization.
-   **[squidpy](https://squidpy.readthedocs.io/)** --- spatial transcriptomics in Python.

## Foundational and review papers

Read these in roughly the order listed; each builds on the previous.

| Paper | Why it matters |
|---|---|
| [Tang *et al.* 2009, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.1315) | The first single-cell RNA-seq experiment. Useful historical context. |
| [Macosko *et al.* 2015, *Cell*](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2015.05.002) | Drop-seq — the droplet-based throughput leap. |
| [Zheng *et al.* 2017, *Nature Communications*](https://doi.org/10.1038/ncomms14049) | The 10x Chromium platform paper; describes the chemistry the workshop dataset uses. |
| [Stuart, Butler *et al.* 2019, *Cell*](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2019.05.031) | Seurat v3 / anchor-based integration. |
| [Wolf, Angerer & Theis 2018, *Genome Biology*](https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-017-1382-0) | Scanpy. |
| [Luecken & Theis 2019, *Mol Sys Bio*](https://doi.org/10.15252/msb.20188746) | "Current best practices in single-cell RNA-seq analysis" — still essential. |
| [Kharchenko 2021, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-021-01171-x) | "The triumphs and limitations of computational methods for scRNA-seq" — a reality check. |
| [Heumos *et al.* 2023, *Nat Rev Genet*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41576-023-00586-w) | Companion paper to the sc-best-practices book; high-level overview. |
| [Squair *et al.* 2021, *Nature Communications*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-25960-2) | Shows why **pseudobulk** is the right default for condition-level DE. |
| [Crowell *et al.* 2020, *Nature Communications*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-020-19894-4) | `muscat` — pseudobulk DE in practice. |
| [Argelaguet *et al.* 2021, *Genome Biology*](https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-021-02496-8) | Multi-omics factor analysis (MOFA+). |
| [Lopez *et al.* 2018, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-018-0229-2) | scVI — variational autoencoder for single-cell data; foundation for scvi-tools. |
| [Korsunsky *et al.* 2019, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-019-0619-0) | Harmony --- still the recommended first integration tool. |
| [Tabula Sapiens consortium 2022, *Science*](https://doi.org/10.1126/science.abl4896) | A reference human atlas — useful both as a reference for annotation and as a model for atlas construction. |

## Topic-specific deep dives

### Annotation & references

-   [SingleR — Aran *et al.* 2019](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41590-018-0276-y) --- automated, reference-based annotation.
-   [Azimuth](https://azimuth.hubmapconsortium.org/) --- web tool for reference-based mapping (human refs).
-   [CellTypist](https://www.celltypist.org/) --- logistic regression models across tissues.
-   [PanglaoDB](https://panglaodb.se/) and [CellMarker 2.0](http://bio-bigdata.hrbmu.edu.cn/CellMarker/) --- canonical marker compendia.

### Trajectory & dynamics

-   [Saelens *et al.* 2019, *Nature Biotechnology*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41587-019-0071-9) --- comparison of 45 trajectory inference methods.
-   [Monocle3](https://cole-trapnell-lab.github.io/monocle3/) and [Slingshot](https://www.bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/html/slingshot.html).
-   [La Manno *et al.* 2018, *Nature*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-018-0414-6) --- RNA velocity (foundational); see also [scVelo](https://scvelo.readthedocs.io/).

### Cell--cell communication

-   [Dimitrov *et al.* 2022, *Nature Communications*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-022-30755-0) --- LIANA: meta-resource benchmarking CCC tools.
-   [Jin *et al.* 2021, *Nature Communications*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-21246-9) --- CellChat.

### Spatial transcriptomics

-   [Moses & Pachter 2022, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-022-01409-2) --- museum/landscape of spatial methods.
-   [Visium / Stereo-seq / MERFISH platform docs](https://www.10xgenomics.com/products/spatial-gene-expression).

### Multi-omics & ATAC-seq

-   [Granja *et al.* 2021, *Nat Genet*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41588-021-00790-6) --- ArchR.
-   [Stuart *et al.* 2021, *Nature Methods*](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-021-01282-5) --- Signac for scATAC-seq.

## Public datasets

Useful for practice, reanalysis, and integration with your own work.

-   **[10x Genomics datasets](https://www.10xgenomics.com/resources/datasets)** --- including the workshop's 20k NSCLC DTC dataset and the PBMC 10k scATAC dataset.
-   **[Tabula Sapiens (human)](https://tabula-sapiens-portal.ds.czbiohub.org/)** and **[Tabula Muris (mouse)](https://tabula-muris.ds.czbiohub.org/)**.
-   **[Human Cell Atlas](https://data.humancellatlas.org/)**.
-   **[GEO](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/)** and **[Single Cell Portal (Broad)](https://singlecell.broadinstitute.org/single_cell)** --- searchable repositories.
-   **[CELLxGENE Discover](https://cellxgene.cziscience.com/)** --- curated, queryable atlases with built-in viewers.

## Cheat sheets

Quick references that pair well with the appendices in [Resources](Resources.qmd).

-   [Base R Cheat Sheet](https://iqss.github.io/dss-workshops/R/Rintro/base-r-cheat-sheet.pdf)
-   [RStudio Collection of Cheat Sheets](https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/)
-   [Git Cheat Sheet](https://education.github.com/git-cheat-sheet-education.pdf)
-   [Markdown Cheat Sheet (Quarto)](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html)
-   [LaTeX Cheat Sheet](https://wch.github.io/latexsheet/)
-   [Seurat command list](https://satijalab.org/seurat/articles/essential_commands.html) --- function-by-function quick reference.

## Communities & courses

-   **[scverse Discourse](https://discourse.scverse.org/)** --- the friendliest place to ask Python single-cell questions.
-   **[Bioconductor support](https://support.bioconductor.org/)** --- for R / Bioconductor questions.
-   **[Single Cell Genomics Day (NYGC)](https://satijalab.org/scgd25/)** --- annual recap of tools and methods.
-   **[Sanger Single-Cell Course](https://www.singlecellcourse.org/)** --- self-paced, free.
